In spite of the opposition to its
proposed subway schemes, the North
ern Pacific Railway company has
started to carry out its original plan
of raising it's tracks and making the
city build subways throughout the
city.


For several days gangs of work.
men have been employed on the right
of way of the road at Madclia street
in tine eastern part of town, where
piles for widening the road to the
extent of IL tracks are now being
driven. City officials say this means
but one thing-that the Northern Pa
cific intends to go ahead with it's
original plan.


In view of the present unsettled
condition of affairs, the action of
the road is looked upon as nervy, and
that the officials of the road have
no fear of losing in their fight


City Engineer Mcintyre says in
case the company should be COM
PEELED to lower it's tracks through
the city 1t would lose al 1.-The work
1t is now pushing so rapidly.


"The company could easily change
Its plans as far as the Sprague ave-
nue. subway is concerned" said Mr.
Mcintyre, were It forced to keep the
tracks at the presnt level. But the
present work would cause 1t much
trouble If 1t were forced to lower
the tracks. as many of the citizens
would like to have 1t do"


The present state of affairs is puz-
cling many of the city officials. While
the railroad is preparing to raise its
tracks the city Is going ahead with
the Browne street crossing.


The city council and board of pub
lie works will meet at the chamber
of commerce this afternoon to dis
cuss the matter. 1t is expected some
of the railroad officials will be pres
ent


During the meeting this afternoon
the report of the chamber of COM
merce committee on the matter of the
Northern Pacific subway was read
The committee recommended that ii
would be better to have the city enei-
necr and one other engineer draw
plans to be submitted in case the
plans of the railway company are not
satisfactory.
